_comment: Tier-1 edge-review verdicts overlay (ROADMAP M3). Default verdict is 'correct'; only non-correct calls are listed. Keyed 'concept>prereq'. Source: adversarial independent review 2026-06-21 (102 correct / 6 arguable / 0 wrong). 'arguable' edges await human expert sign-off (METHODOLOGY §13).

term>object.note: a term 'denotes an object' only under an interpretation; at the pure syntax level a term denotes nothing, so this defined-via link leans on semantics.

satisfaction>sentence.note: satisfaction is defined over formulas+assignments first; 'sentence' is the derived assignment-independent special case, so as a prerequisite it slightly inverts the definitional order.

recursively-enumerable>decidable.note: r.e. is *weaker* than decidable, so kind `refines` (connoting strengthening) points the wrong way, though learning decidable first is reasonable.

primitive-recursive>decidable.note: true as containment (PR ⊆ decidable), but PR is usually introduced before general decidability/Turing-computability, so decidable-as-prerequisite is pedagogically backwards.

representable>primitive-recursive.note: 'PR relations are representable' is a theorem, not the *definition* of representability; kind `defined-via` frames a theorem as a definition.

representable>arithmetization.note: representability is defined via *provability in T* (T ⊢), not via arithmetization; arithmetization motivates but doesn't define it. Strongest candidate for a future refinement (tie representable to provability).
